description_long: This is a medium-sized antelope with a slender, athletic build and a short, reddish-brown coat that transitions to white on the underbelly and inner legs. Its most distinctive features are the long, ringed, lyre-shaped horns that curve backward, present on both sides of the head. The face is marked by a white blaze running from the nose up between the eyes, bordered by darker brown patches. The legs are long and thin, adapted for running, with black markings on the front legs and a short, tufted tail. The animal's ears are pointed and alert, and its overall appearance suggests agility and alertness, typical of species adapted to open grasslands or savannas. This animal would be an unusual but striking presence in an urban park or green space.
description: A slender, reddish-brown antelope with long, curved horns, white underbelly, and distinctive facial markings. Its athletic build and alert posture suggest agility and speed.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical, curved horns, pointed ears, and white facial blaze.
description_view_1: Left side profile highlighting the full body length, reddish-brown coat, and the curve of the horns.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the short tail, white underbelly, and the back of the horns.
description_view_3: Right side profile similar to the left, showing the body shape, horn curvature, and leg markings.
